{{Quote|I'm not the Sarlacc?|Susejo|<ref name="ABLT:TToBF" />}}
'''Susejo''' was the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]] of the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]]. Formerly a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Choi (species)|Choi]], Susejo fell victim to the Great Pit of Carkoon on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] before his [[Sentience/Legends|conciousness]] was merged with that of the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]].<ref name="ABLT:TToBF" />

The very [[Time/Legends|young]] Susejo fell into the sarlacc pit around four thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. After being swallowed by the sarlacc, he passed time by trading stories with [[Unidentified female Jedi (sarlacc)|another Jedi]] who shared his fate. By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], his personality had merged with that of the sarlacc, which had digested him alive for thousands of years whilst it absorbed his consciousness. Thus, Susejo's personality embodied the creature, rendering it indistinguishable from the ancient sentient. Susejo introduced himself to [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] when the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] suffered the same fate.<ref name="ABLT:TToBF" />

Throughout Fett's stay, Susejo/the sarlacc and Fett had several lengthy conversations. It was by goading Susejo that Fett made the sarlacc contract around his [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]], creating a means of escape.<ref name="ABLT:TToBF" />

Fett returned a [[5 ABY/Legends|year]] after his escape to taunt Susejo and the sarlacc, promising to eventually destroy them one [[Standard day/Legends|day]].<ref name="ABLT:TToBF" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Then they told me the Sarlacc couldn't be intelligent, which was the actual center of the weakened story, so I took all the Sarlacc's contribution to the story and gave it to one of Fett's fellow prisoners "Susejo," or O Jesus backwards|[[Daniel Keys Moran]] on Susejo's creation|<ref name="Moran interview">{{WebCite|url=https://starwarsinterviews.com/various/authors/daniel-keys-moran-author|text=Daniel Keys Moran interview|archivedate=20200817023040|nolive=1}}</ref>}}
Susejo made his first, and only, appearance in the [[1996|1995]] short story "[[A Barve Like That: The Tale of Boba Fett]]," written by [[Daniel Keys Moran]] under the pseudonym of "[[Daniel Keys Moran|J.D. Montgomery]]" and published as part of the ''[[Tales from Jabba's Palace]]'' anthology.<ref name="ABLT:TToBF" /> Keys Moran initially intended for the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]] to be [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], but ultimately gave its contribution to the story to Susejo when [[Lucasfilm]] vetoed the possibility of the sarlacc being intelligent.<ref name="Moran interview" />
